title: Maternal and offspring fasting glucose and type 2 diabetes-associated genetic variants and cognitive function at age 8: a Mendelian randomization study in the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children.
ehr_evidence: The study uses the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children (ALSPAC) cohort, which is a birth cohort study, not an EHR.
summary: A Mendelian randomization study investigating whether maternal and offspring genetic risk scores for fasting glucose and type 2 diabetes are causally linked to child intelligence quotient (IQ) at age 8.
key_findings: No association found between maternal or offspring fasting glucose genetic risk scores (FGGRS) and child IQ.
Maternal type 2 diabetes genetic risk score (T2DGRS) was positively associated with offspring IQ.
Suggests no causal link between in utero glucose exposure and childhood IQ.
population: Children at age 8 in the ALSPAC cohort
